compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

`.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` already has the full key-materialize + job-level
signing wiring (story 9e76/cc0b): it reads the `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` secret, masks it
(`::add-mask::`), writes it to a `0600` file at `${RUNNER_TEMP}/rebar-bot-signing-key`, and
exports `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY=<that path>` (`.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml:211-228`),
with the bot email defaulted to `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` (`:205`, `:267`) so reconciler
events resolve to identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`. The wiring is **secret-gated**: with the
secret empty it writes **unsigned/advisory** (`:220-222`). This story confirms CI signs in
steady state — provisioning + verification of existing wiring, not new plumbing. It does **not**
modify the workflow YAML.

## Precondition (verified, not created)

- Identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d` (Rebar Bot) already exists (one registered ed25519 key); CI
  resolves to it via the `BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL` default. Same identity local signing (79da) uses.
- The canonical private key lives in AWS SSM SecureString `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key`
  (ADR-0012); its public half is exactly the key registered on 594c (verified locally by
  `ssh-keygen -y` match during 79da).
- A `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` secret already exists (`gh secret list`, last set 2026-07-14), but a
  GitHub secret value cannot be read back, so its correctness is only *proven* by a run that
  signs — not by its presence.

## What

1. **Set the secret from the authoritative SSM source.** `gh secret set REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY
   --repo navapbc/rebar` fed the decrypted SSM value (piped, never echoed). This guarantees the
   secret holds the key whose public half is registered on 594c. **T4 (irreversible overwrite):
   this is safe and recoverable — SSM is the single source of truth (ADR-0012) and is left
   unchanged; the "rollback" for the write-only secret is simply to re-run `gh secret set` from
   the same SSM param.** Record the `gh secret list` timestamp.
2. **Prove CI signs.** Capture `C=$(git -C .tickets-tracker rev-parse HEAD)`, trigger a
   `reconcile-bridge` run (`workflow_dispatch`), wait for it to land ticket writes (fetch
   `origin/tickets`), and verify the events it introduced are signed + verified.

## Scope

The GitHub Actions workflow that writes tickets (`reconcile-bridge`). Secret set from SSM +
one live signing proof. No enforcement flip (8d39); no workflow-YAML change; empty `file_impact`
(no tracked repo-file change).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **The signing secret is set from SSM.** `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` on `navapbc/rebar` is set
      from `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` (whose public half is registered on 594c).
      Evidence recorded: the `gh secret set` success + the `gh secret list` timestamp line.
- [ ] **A CI run produces signed, verified bot events.** With `C` = the tickets-branch HEAD
      captured before a `reconcile-bridge` run, after the run lands its writes on `origin/tickets`:
      (a) **non-vacuous** — the run advanced the tickets branch with bot-authored commits:
      `git -C .tickets-tracker rev-list "$C"..origin/tickets --count` > 0 **and**
      `git -C .tickets-tracker log "$C"..origin/tickets --format='%ae' | sort -u` contains
      `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`; and (b) **every non-exempt event the run introduced verifies
      under enforcement**: `rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated --since "$C"`
      exits 0 (an unsigned in-scope bot write would make this non-zero — this is exactly the
      enforcement the flip applies). Record `C`, the run URL, and the new commit range.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The secret is set in GitHub settings and a real run proves signing.
      Evidence recorded as a comment: the workflow-run URL, `C`, the new tickets commit range,
      the `verify-identity --since C` exit-0 result, and the `gh secret list` timestamp.

file_impact.reason: CI signing wiring this story provisions + verifies

compiled_state.title: Populate REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY and confirm GitHub Actions produces signed ticket events
